      Recipients with this status cannot receive email campaigns or opt-in messages. Recipients with this status can only opt in again through the Customer Center or by clicking the link in a campaign message they have received prior to opting out.

fields.label: Home Phone
fields.help: Enter the contact's home phone number. This number appears only on this record.

fields.label: Image
fields.help:
Select an image from your file cabinet to attach to this record.

Select -New- to upload a new image from your hard drive to your file cabinet in a new window.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help:
When you check this box, this contact no longer appears on the Contacts list unless you check the Show Inactives box at the bottom of the page.

Also, you can no longer select this contact from lists on transactions, company records, task records or events records.

fields.label: Private
fields.help:
Check this box if this is a private contact.

Private contacts can only be viewed by the person that entered the contact record. They are also excluded from the Duplicate Detection process.

Enter the contact's last name.

What you enter here automatically appears last in the Contact field.

This field is required for the Online Bill Pay feature.

Enter the contact's middle name or initial. This field is optional.

What you enter here automatically appears second in the Contact field.

fields.label: Mobile Phone
fields.help: Enter the contact's mobile or cell phone number. This number appears only on this record.

fields.label: Office Phone
fields.help: Enter the contact's work phone number. This number appears only on this record.

fields.label: Other Relationships
fields.help: If there are other records in your account for this individual or company, they are listed here.

fields.label: Cell Phone
fields.help: Enter the phone number you primarily use to reach this contact. This number appears in the Contacts list and in the Contact section of company records. This field is required for the Online Bill Pay feature.

fields.label: blank
fields.help: Enter the furigana character you want to use to sort this record.

fields.label: Mr/Mrs/Ms
fields.help:
Enter the contact's salutation.

Examples are Mr., Mrs., Ms. and Miss.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help:
Select the subsidiary to associate with this contact. You cannot enter transactions for this contact unless a subsidiary is assigned.
 
If you select this contact on a transaction, the transaction is associated with this subsidiary. The contact is able to access only information associated with this subsidiary.
 
Note: Once a transaction has posted for the contact, you are not able to change the subsidiary selected on the contact record.

fields.label: Supervisor
fields.help:
Select this contact's supervisor.

You can select only from existing contacts. To add to this list, create another contact record.

fields.label: Sup. Phone
fields.help: Enter the phone number for this contact's supervisor.

fields.label: Job Title
fields.help:
Enter this contact's title at his or her company.

On the record for this contact's company, in the Contacts section, this title appears next to the contacts name.

fields.label: Unsubscribe from Campaigns
fields.help:
This box is checked if this contact has unsubscribed from your e-mail marketing campaigns. Unsubscribed contacts receive no marketing campaign e-mail.

Contacts can unsubscribe to your e-mail marketing campaigns by clicking a link in any campaign e-mail they receive.

To resubscribe to e-mail campaigns, a contact must opt in through the Customer Center or click the Unsubscribe link on a campaign e-mail message.

If you are using the US Edition of NetSuite and you want new contacts to be subscribed by default, an administrator can go to Setup > Marketing > Set Up Marketing and clear the Unsubscribed to Marketing by Default box.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help: Check this box to inactivate this contact category.

fields.label: Contact Category
fields.help: Enter the name for this contact category.

fields.label: Private
fields.help: Check this box if you want to be the only one to use this contact category.

fields.label: Description
fields.help: Enter a description of this note type.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help: Check this box to inactivate this contact category.

fields.label: Role
fields.help: Enter the name for this contact category.

fields.label: Expense Account
fields.help:
Select the appropriate default account to assign.

If you are entering a payment method, select the default bank account for this payment method. Then, the account autofills the Deposit To field on transactions when this payment method is selected.

If you are creating a landed cost category, select the expense account to be used as a clearing account for the landed cost of items. Then, when the item is sold, the cost of goods sold is accurately reflected. This field shows only for landed cost category records, not for service or material types.

If you are creating a project expense type select which account you want debited when posting project time transactions. 

Note: If you enable the Expand Accounts preference, you can choose any account, not just bank accounts or expense accounts, when you are creating a landed cost category or a project expense type. 

Enabling the Expand Accounts preference has no effect when you are entering a payment method.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help:
Check this box to remove all references to this record from your account.

You can reactivate the record at any time.

fields.label: Cost Type
fields.help:
In the Cost Type field, select one of the following:
    * Landed
    * Material
    * Service
Note: The option for Landed shows only if you have enabled the Landed Cost feature.

fields.label: Name
fields.help: Enter the name for this record. This name appears in lists that include this record.

fields.label: Coupon Code
fields.help: The coupon code that is entered on a sales transaction to apply the promotion. Coupon codes can be multiple-use or single-use. Multiple-use codes can be used any number of times by any number of customers. Each single-use code can only be used in one transaction by a single customer.

fields.label: Date Sent
fields.help: This field shows the date the coupon code was sent to the recipient.

fields.label: Promotion
fields.help: This field displays the name of the promotion that the selected coupon code is used with.

fields.label: Recipient
fields.help:
This field displays the recipient of the selected coupon code.

Note that the recipient of the coupon code may differ from the customer who used the coupon code.

fields.label: Used
fields.help: The coupon has been used by the recipient when the Used box is checked.

fields.label: Account
fields.help:
This is the credit card account for this transaction. In edit mode, you can select an existing account or set up a new account by clicking New.

For details about existing accounts, go to Setup > Accounts.

fields.label: Balance
fields.help: NetSuite displays the balance in this account.

fields.label: Class
fields.help:
This is the class with which this credit card transaction is associated. In edit mode, you can select an existing class or create a new class by selecting New.

For details on existing classes, go to Setup > Company > Classes.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: This field defaults to the currency associated with the selected vendor.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help:
This is the type of form used to enter credit card transaction information in NetSuite. You can select either a standard or custom form.

To create your own custom form, select an existing standard form and customize it. For more information, see Custom Forms.

fields.label: Department
fields.help:
This is the department with which this credit card transaction is associated. In edit mode, you can select an existing department or create a new department by selecting New.

For details on existing departments, go to Setup > Company > Departments.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.help:
This is the vendor you are paying or the vendor who is crediting you for a previous purchase. In edit mode, you can select an existing vendor or set up a new vendor by clicking New.

For details about existing vendors, go to Lists > Relationships > Vendors.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help:
NetSuite displays the currency's exchange rate in this field.

You can edit the exchange rate for this transaction only, or you can update the currency record with the exchange rate you enter here.

fields.label: Location
fields.help:
This is the location with which this credit card transaction is associated. In edit mode, you can select an existing location or create a new location by selecting New.

For details on existing locations, go to Setup > Company > Locations.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: This is the memo that will appear as a description for this credit card transaction on reports. If you want, enter a memo.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help:
This is the period to which you want to post this credit card transaction.

You only need to select a posting period if you use accounting periods. If a period is closed, you cannot post to that period.

For details about accounting periods, see Accounting Period Management.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help:
This field defaults to the primary subsidiary associated with the selected vendor.

If you use NetSuite OneWorld and the selected vendor is shared with multiple subsidiaries, you can select any of the secondary subsidiaries assigned to this vendor. If you enter line items and then change the subsidiary, all line item data will be cleared. .

fields.label: Date
fields.help:
NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this credit card transaction. You can type or pick another date.

What you enter here determines the date range in which this transaction appears on the Credit Card Register.

You can select or enter custom date ranges on registers.

fields.label: Reference #
fields.help:
This is the transaction number from your credit card transaction. If required, enter the applicable transaction number.

The maximum number of characters you can enter in this field is 45.

fields.label: Transaction Number
fields.help:
If you use the automatic transaction numbering feature, this field displays To Be Generated. Auto-generated numbers for transaction records are internal, gapless numbers that cannot be overwritten and are generated when the record is saved. These internal numbers are generated for each transaction type. For more information, see Auto-Generated Transaction Numbers.

If you do not use the automatic transaction numbering feature, enter a transaction number for this transaction.

fields.label: Charge
fields.help:
This indicates whether this credit card transaction is a charge or credit. If you are making a purchase, choose Charge. To record a credit to your account, choose Credit.

After you save the transaction, you cannot change its type.

fields.label: Amount
fields.help:
This is the dollar amount of the transaction. You can type the dollar amount of the transaction, but NetSuite updates the amount as you add or edit line items.

If you choose Credit, credits appear as positive numbers.

fields.label: Account
fields.help:
Select the credit card account for this transaction.

To set up a new credit card account, click New.

For details about existing accounts, go to Setup > Accounts.

fields.label: Balance
fields.help: NetSuite displays the balance in this account.

fields.label: Class
fields.help:
Select a class to associate with this transaction.

To create a new class, select New.

For details on existing classes, go to <_TABNAME=LIST_CLAS_> > <_TASKCATEGORY=LIST_CLASS_> > Classes

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: This field defaults to the currency associated with the selected vendor.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help: Select the custom form for this credit card transaction.

fields.label: Department
fields.help:
Select a department to associate with this transaction.

To create a new department, select New.

For details on existing departments, go to <_TABNAME=LIST_DEPARTMENT_> > <_TASKCATEGORY=LIST_DEPARTMENT_> > Departments.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.help:
Select the vendor you are paying or the vendor who is crediting you for a previous purchase.

To set up a new vendor, click New.

For details about existing vendors, Lists > <_TASKCATEGORY=taskID_> Vendor.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help:
NetSuite displays the currency's exchange rate in this field.

You can edit the exchange rate for this transaction only, or you can update the currency record with the exchange rate you enter here.

fields.label: Location
fields.help:
Select a location to associate with this item.

Select New to create a new location record.

Go to Lists > Locations for details about existing locations.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: If you want, type a memo to describe this credit card transaction. It will appear on reports.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help:
Select the period to which you want to post this credit card transaction.

If a period is closed, you cannot post to that period.

fields.label: Date
fields.help:
NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this credit card transaction. You can type or pick another date.

What you enter here determines the date range in which this transaction appears on the Credit Card Register.

You can select or enter custom date ranges on registers.

fields.label: Reference #
fields.help:
If required, type the transaction number from your credit card transaction.

The maximum number of characters you can enter in this field is 45.

fields.label: Transaction Number
fields.help: If you use the automatic transaction numbering feature, this field displays To Be Generated. If you do not use the feature, enter a transaction number for this transaction.

fields.label: Charge
fields.help:
If you are making a purchase, choose Charge.

To record a credit to your account, choose Credit.

After you save the transaction, you cannot change its type.

fields.label: Amount
fields.help:
You can type the dollar amount of the transaction, but NetSuite updates the amount as you add or edit line items.

If you choose Credit, credits appear as positive numbers.

fields.label: Account
fields.help: Select the Accounts Receivable account that will be affected by this transaction. Click New to set up a new account.

fields.label: Handling Cost
fields.help: The handling cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field. To change the cost of handling, go to Lists > Shipping Items and select the shipping method with the handling cost you want to change.

fields.label: Shipping Cost
fields.help: The shipping cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field above.

fields.label: Applied
fields.help: NetSuite displays the amount of credit you applied below.

fields.label: Auto Apply
fields.help: Check this box to automatically apply payments.

fields.label: Bill To
fields.help: The default billing address autofills this field from the customer's record.

fields.label: Bill To Select
fields.help: Select the appropriate billing address for this transaction.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: Select a class to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Coupon Code
fields.help: Type a coupon code for this customer credit.

fields.help: Select the transaction currency.

fields.help: This customer's currency is shown in this field.

fields.label: Custom Form

fields.label: Deferred Revenue

fields.label: Discount
fields.help: You can select a discount for this credit memo.

fields.label: Rate
fields.help: NetSuite enters the rate for the discount item you selected.

fields.label: Discount
fields.help: NetSuite enters the amount discounted on this credit memo.

fields.help: Select the customer for this credit.

fields.help: Estimated Gross Profit Margin, as a percentage.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help: The currency's exchange rate is shown in this field.

fields.label: Exclude Commissions

fields.label: Handling Cost

fields.label: Handling Tax Rate
fields.help: The tax rate for this tax code is shown here.

fields.label: Handling Tax Code
fields.help: Select the tax code that determines the tax for handling charges.

fields.label: Enable Item Line Shipping
fields.help: Check this box to allow shipping to more than one address.

fields.label: Taxable
fields.help: Check this box if the order being credited is taxable.

fields.label: Lead Source
fields.help: Select the lead source this transaction is associated with.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Select a location to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: If you wish, enter a memo to describe this credit.

fields.label: Customer Message
fields.help: Select a customer message that will appear on the Credit Memo form.

fields.label: Select Message
fields.help: Select a customer message to appear on this form.

fields.label: Nexus
fields.help: This field shows the nexus of the transaction.

fields.label: PO #
fields.help: For your customers' convenience, you can enter their purchase order number here.

fields.label: Partner
fields.help: The partner associated with this customer appears in the Partner field.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help: Select the period you want to post this credit memo to.

fields.label: Promotion
fields.help: Select a promotion code to apply to this transaction.

fields.label: Recognized Revenue
fields.help: Recognized Revenue: cumulative amount of revenue recognized for this transaction

fields.label: Revenue Status
fields.help: The possible values: Pending, In Progress, Completed.

fields.label: Rev Rec on Rev Commit.
fields.help: Check this check box to create a Revenue Commitment or Revenue Commitment Reversal.

fields.help: You can change the sales effective date for this transaction.

fields.label: Shipping Tax Rate

fields.help: This field shows the tax registration number of the transaction nexus.

fields.label: Subtotal

fields.label: Update Customer

fields.label: Update Customer
fields.help: Check this box to update the sales team on the customer's record.

fields.help: Check this box to override the tax information.

fields.label: Tax
fields.help: NetSuite inserts the tax item for this customer.

fields.label: Tax %
fields.help: NetSuite inserts the percentage rate of the tax item.

fields.label: Tax Registration Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the values in the Nexus and Subsidiary Tax Reg. Number fields.

fields.label: Tax Total
fields.help: NetSuite multiplies the tax rate by the taxable total.

fields.label: To Be Faxed

fields.help: Check this box to save this in a queue of credit memos to print.

fields.label: Total
fields.help: NetSuite computes the total of line items and tax.

fields.label: Est. Extended Cost
