fields.help: Read-only field that shows the invoice group number for this invoice.

fields.label: Handling Cost
fields.help:
The handling cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field.

To change the cost of handling, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items and select the shipping method with the handling cost you want to change.

fields.label: Handling Tax Rate
fields.help: The tax rate for the tax code selected shows here.

fields.label: Handling Tax Code
fields.help: Select the tax code that determines the tax for handling charges.

fields.label: Enable Item Line Shipping
fields.help: Check this box to enable line-item shipping. For example, you may have line items that cannot be shipped together.

fields.label: Taxable
fields.help: Place a check mark in this box if the order you are invoicing is taxable.

fields.label: Amount
fields.help:
The rate you enter automatically appears here.

If you entered a percentage in the Rate field, NetSuite multiplies the discount or markup percentage by the total amount of line items you select.

fields.label: Discount/Markup
fields.help:
If you want to add a discount or markup to the cost of the items you are billing, select it from the list.

To see a list of your discounts or markups, go to Lists > Items and select Discount or Markup from the dropdown list at the bottom of the page.

To create a new discount or markup, go to Lists > New Item and click Discount or Markup.

fields.label: Print
fields.help: Check this box if you added a discount or markup and want to print it on the invoice.

fields.label: Rate
fields.help:
The rate for the discount or markup item you selected automatically appears here.

If you want, enter a new rate.

Rates can be entered as dollar amounts and percentages.

fields.label: Apply Before Tax
fields.help: Check this box if you want the discount or markup you entered to be applied before taxes.

fields.label: Tax Rate
fields.help:
NetSuite inserts the GST rate based on the tax code you assigned to this item.

To set up tax codes and corresponding tax percentages, go to Lists > Tax Codes.

To change the assignment of a tax code to an item, go to Lists > Items.

fields.label: Lead Source
fields.help:
Select the lead source associated with this transaction.

If you use the Marketing Automation feature, select the campaign you want to associate with this transaction's revenue.

fields.label: Location
fields.help:
Choose the location you want to associate with this transaction.

Select New to enter a new location record.

Go to Lists > Locations for details about existing location records.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help:
Enter an optional memo to describe this invoice so you can easily distinguish it from others for this customer.

For example: Suite A installation or Suite B installation.

It will appear on reports such as the 2-line Accounts Receivable register, which your customers can see if you give them permission to log in and view their transaction history.

fields.label: Customer Message
fields.help:
The text of the customer message you select in the Select Message field appears here.

You can edit the message or enter a new customer message for only this transaction.

fields.label: Select Message
fields.help:
Select a customer message to appear on this form. For example, you may add "Thank you for your business!" Choose from the list of existing customer messages stored in Accounting Lists.

The text of the selected message appears in the Customer Message field. In this field, you can optionally edit the message or enter a new customer message for only this transaction.

To add messages to appear in the Select Message field, go to Setup > Accounting > Accounting Lists > New, and click Customer Message.

fields.label: Nexus
fields.help:
This field shows the nexus of the transaction. NetSuite automatically populates this field based on the nexus lookup logic. 

You can override the transaction nexus and tax registration number that NetSuite automatically selects by checking the Nexus Override box. When you select a different nexus in the dropdown list, the corresponding tax registration number is automatically selected in the Subsidiary Tax Reg. Number field.

fields.label: Opportunity
fields.help:
If you are entering this transaction for an opportunity, select the opportunity.

You can create opportunity records at Transactions > Sales > Create Opportunities.

fields.label: PO #
fields.help: To make it easy for your customers to locate this invoice in their own records, enter their purchase order number here.

fields.label: Partner
fields.help:
The partner associated with this customer appears in the Partner field.

 

You can change the partner for this transaction.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help:
Select the period you want to post this invoice to.

If a period is closed, you cannot post to that period.

If your transaction is subject to approval, the posting period is set when the transaction is approved. For details, refer to Posting Period for Transactions Subject to Approval under the help topic Approval Routing.

fields.label: Promotion
fields.help:
If you want to apply a multiple-use coupon code to this transaction, select the promotion here.

If you want to apply a single-use coupon code, enter the code in the Coupon Code field. The promotion for that coupon code is shown here.

Customers associated with a partner can only use coupon codes from that partner's promotions. If you select a promotion associated with a partner, this transaction is attributed to that partner for reporting purposes.

You can choose to have a promotion selected by default at Setup > Marketing > Marketing Preferences in the Default Promotion Code on Sales Transactions field.

Create new promotion codes at Lists > Marketing > Promotions > New.

fields.label: Recognized Revenue
fields.help: Recognized Revenue: cumulative amount of revenue recognized for this transaction

fields.label: Recurring Bill
fields.help: Check the Recurring Bill box to create a recurring invoice for the billing account.

fields.label: Revenue Status
fields.help:
The possible values for this field are:

   1. Pending: indicates that no recognition has occurred. All revenue is still deferred.
   2. In Progress: indicates that some recognition has occurred.
   3. Completed: indicates that all recognition has occurred. No deferred revenue remains.

fields.label: Rev Rec on Rev Commit.
fields.help:
Check this check box to create a Revenue Commitment or Revenue Commitment Reversal.

    * On a Sales Order, the Revenue Commitment replaces an invoice
    * On a Return Authorization, the Revenue Commitment Reversal replaces a credit memo.

NetSuite creates all applicable Revenue Recognition schedules the Revenue Commitment or Revenue Commitment Reversal.

fields.label: Sales Effective Date
fields.help: You can change the sales effective date for this transaction. The sales effective date determines which commission plan and historical sales team this transaction applies to.

fields.label: Choose Team
fields.help:
Select a sales team to associate with this transaction.

To create a sales team, go to Lists > Relationships > New > Select Sales Team Members.

fields.label: Sales Rep
fields.help:
NetSuite inserts the sales representative associated with this company on the customer record at Lists > Customers.

If a different sales rep is assigned to this transaction, select that sales rep here.

To add additional sales reps to this list, go to Lists > New Employee.

fields.label: Ship To
fields.help:
The default shipping address autofills this field from the customer's record.

To enter a different address:

    * Select another address in the Ship To Select field.
    * Select New in the Ship To Select field to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record.
    * Select Custom in the Ship To Select field to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record).
    * Click the Edit icon for the Ship To Select field to edit an existing shipping address.

fields.label: Ship To Select
fields.help:
Select the appropriate shipping address for this transaction. 

    * Select New to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record.
    * Select Custom to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record).
    * Click the Edit icon to edit an existing shipping address.

fields.label: Ship Date
fields.help: Type or pick a shipping date for this invoice.

fields.label: Shipping Method
fields.help:
Select a shipping method for this order.

If you automatically charge for shipping, the shipping amount is entered in the shipping costs field. To automatically charge for shipping, go to Setup > Accounting > Shipping. Check the Charge for Shipping box and click Save. You must also set up the charge details of your shipping items.

To add or edit shipping items, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items.

fields.label: Shipping Cost
fields.help:
The shipping cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field above.

To change the cost of a shipping method, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items and select the shipping method you want to change.

If you use UPS Real-Time rates, shipments over 150lbs are broken up into shipments less than or equal to 150lbs for charging.

fields.label: Shipping Tax Rate
fields.help: The tax rate for the selected tax code shows here.

fields.label: Shipping Tax Code
fields.help: Select the tax code that determines the tax charged for shipping.

fields.label: Source
fields.help: The source of the invoice, for example, the program or software name.

fields.label: Start Date
fields.help:
If you use the Advanced Billing feature, enter the date for the first invoice to be created.

If an invoice is created from a sales order that includes a Start Date and End Date, those dates autofill on the invoice when the order is billed.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: This field displays the subsidiary associated with this transaction.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help:
This field shows the tax registration number of the transaction nexus. NetSuite automatically populates this field based on the nexus lookup logic. 

You can override the transaction nexus and tax registration number that NetSuite automatically selects by checking the Nexus Override box. When you select a different tax registration number in the dropdown list, the corresponding nexus is automatically selected in the Nexus field.

fields.label: Subtotal
fields.help:
This field shows the total of the items on this invoice.

This is the total before any discounts, shipping cost, handling cost or tax is added to the order.

fields.label: Update Customer
fields.help: Check this box if you want to update the sales team on the customer's record with changes you make here.

fields.label: Update Customer
fields.help: Check this box if you want to update the sales team on the customer's record with changes you make here.

fields.label: Tax Details Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the tax information on the Tax Details subtab of the transaction. Only roles with at least the Edit level of the Tax Details Tab permission can override the tax details.

fields.label: Tax
fields.help:
A tax item is a tax that you collect at one certain rate and pay to one tax agency for this invoice. NetSuite inserts the tax item for this customer from the record at Lists > Customers. To change it just for this order, select another tax item.

To add additional choices to this list, go to Lists > New Item > Sales Tax Item.

fields.label: Tax %
fields.help: NetSuite inserts the percentage rate of the tax item from its record at Lists > Items. You can change it here for just this transaction if you wish.

fields.label: Tax Registration Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the values in the Nexus and Subsidiary Tax Reg. Number fields. Only roles with at least the Edit level of the Tax Details Tab permission can override the values that NetSuite automatically selects in these fields.

fields.label: Tax Total
fields.help: NetSuite multiplies the tax rate by the taxable total of line items and enters it here.

fields.label: Terms
fields.help:
Select billing terms that apply to this invoice.

To add additional choices to this list, go to Setup > Accounting > Accounting Lists > New > Terms.

fields.label: Amount
fields.help:
The rate you enter automatically appears here.

If you entered a percentage in the Rate field, NetSuite multiplies the discount or markup percentage by the total amount of line items you select.

fields.label: Discount/Markup
fields.help:
If you want to add a discount or markup to the cost of the time you are billing, select it from the list.

To see a list of your discounts or markups, go to Lists > Items and select Discount or Markup from the dropdown list at the bottom of the page.

To create a new discount or markup, go to Lists > New Item and click Discount or Markup.

fields.label: Print
fields.help: Check this box if you added a discount or markup and want to print it on the invoice.

fields.label: Rate
fields.help:
The rate for the discount or markup item you selected automatically appears here.

If you want, enter a new rate.

Rates can be entered as dollar amounts and percentages.

fields.label: Apply Before Tax
fields.help: Check this box if you want the discount or markup you entered to be applied before taxes.

fields.label: Tax Rate
fields.help:
NetSuite inserts the GST rate based on the tax code you assigned to this time.

To set up tax codes and corresponding tax percentages, go to Lists > Tax Codes.

To change the assignment of a tax code to an item, go to Lists > Items.

fields.label: To Be E-mailed
fields.help:
When processing individual invoices:
    * Check the To Be Emailed box if you want to email this invoice. Then enter the email address in the space to the right of the check box.
    * You can enter multiple email addresses by separating them with a semicolon. It is important to separate the addresses with only a semicolon, and do not include any spaces.

When bulk processing invoices:

In the To Be Emailed field:
    * Select Yes if you want to email this form.
    * Select No if you do not want to email this form.
    * Select Respect Customer Preference to send the form based on the default preference on the customer record. Set the preference on the Info subtab of a customer record in the Send Transactions Via field.

fields.label: To Be Faxed
fields.help:
When processing individual invoices:

    * Check the To Be Faxed box if you want to fax this invoice. Then enter the fax number in the box to the right of the check box if it does not already appear.


When bulk processing invoices:
In the To Be Faxed field:

    * Select Yes if you want to fax this form.
    * Select No if you do not want to fax this form.
    * Select Respect Customer Preference to send the form based on the default preference on the customer record.
Set the preference on the Info subtab of a customer record in the Send Transactions Via field.

To fax NetSuite forms, an administrator must first set up fax service at Setup > Company > Printing, Fax and Email Preferences.

fields.label: To Be Printed
fields.help:
When processing individual invoices:

Check the To Be Printed box if you want to print this invoice.

When bulk processing invoices:

    * Select Yes if you want to save this in a queue of invoices to print.
    * Select No if you do not want to print this form.
    * Select Respect Customer Preference to send the form based on the default preference on the customer record.
      Set the preference on the Info subtab of a customer record in the Send Transactions Via field.

fields.label: Total
fields.help: NetSuite computes the total of line items, tax and shipping costs, and then enters it here.

fields.label: Est. Extended Cost
fields.help: Estimated Cost: Estimated cost of the specific number of items; estimated rate x quantity = estimated cost.

fields.label: Additional Tracking #
fields.help:
Enter the shipment tracking number from the shipping company.

If a tracking number is entered in the exact format used by UPS or FedEx and the transaction is saved, the tracking number will show on the transaction as a link to the UPS or FedEx website. You can click the link to check the status of the shipment.

fields.label: Date
fields.help:
NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this invoice. You can type or pick another date.

What you enter here determines the date range in which this transaction appears on the Accounts Receivable Register.

You can select or enter custom date ranges on registers.

fields.label: Invoice #
fields.help: NetSuite increases the largest invoice number by one. You can type another number. The next invoice number will revert to the standard pattern.

fields.label: Transaction Is VSOE Bundle
fields.help: Check this box to include all items added to this transaction as a VSOE bundle.

fields.label: Auto Calculate VSOE Allocation
fields.help:
The VSOE allocation amount for a sale can be calculated automatically by checking the Auto Calculate VSOE Allocation box.
 
Allocation is the process to determine the VSOE price for items on a sale. The total VSOE amount allocated is the revenue amount to be recognized for the bundle.

fields.label: Amount Due
fields.help: Shows the total unpaid amount in this invoice group.

fields.label: Amount Paid
fields.help: Displays the total amount paid for this invoice group.

fields.label: Bill To
fields.help:
The default billing address autofills this field from the customer's record.

To enter a different address:

    * Select another address in the Bill To Select field.
    * Select New in the Bill To Select field to enter a new billing address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record.
    * Select Custom in the Bill To Select field to enter a new billing address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record).
    * Click the Edit icon for the Bill To Select field to modify an existing billing address.

fields.label: Bill To Select
fields.help:
Select the appropriate billing address for this transaction. 

    * Select New to enter a new billing address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record.
    * Select Custom to enter a new billing address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record).
    * Click the Edit icon to modify an existing billing address.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: Read-only field that shows the currency for this invoice group.

fields.label: Customer
fields.help: Read-only field that shows the customer for this invoice group.

fields.label: Discount Total
fields.help: Shows the total amount of discounts from the invoices in this invoice group.

fields.label: Due Date
fields.help:
Invoices within this group follow the due date specified in this field. This date is calculated by applying the term on the transaction date.

To edit the due date, you can type or pick a different date for this invoice group.

fields.label: Grouped By PO #
fields.help: Read-only field to determine whether invoices are grouped by purchase order number.

fields.label: Invoice Group Number
fields.help: This is an autogenerated number. NetSuite increases the largest invoice group number by one.

fields.label: Item Total
fields.help: Shows the total amount of items from the invoices in this invoice group.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Enter an optional memo to describe this invoice group so you can distinguish it from others for this customer.

fields.label: PO #
fields.help: Read-only field that shows the PO # associated with this invoice group.

fields.label: Shipping Cost
fields.help:
The shipping cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field above.

To change the cost of a shipping method, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items and select the shipping method you want to change.

If you use UPS Real-Time rates, shipments over 150lbs are broken up into shipments less than or equal to 150lbs for charging.

fields.label: Status
fields.help:
Status of the invoice group.

The status can be any of the following:

   1. Undefined
   2. Open
   3. Partially Paid
   4. Fully Paid
   5. Void

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: Read-only field that shows the subsidiary associated with this invoice group.

fields.label: Tax
fields.help: NetSuite multiplies the tax rate by the taxable total of line items and enters it here.

fields.label: Terms
fields.help: Read-only field that shows the terms that apply to this invoice group.

fields.label: Total
fields.help: NetSuite computes the total of line items, tax and shipping costs, and then enters it here.

fields.label: Date
fields.help: NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this invoice group. You can type or pick another date.

invoicegroup.scriptingNotes: Available in Records Browser but not in SuiteScript Supported Records

searchFilters.label: Accounting Book Rev Rec Forecast Rule
Accounting Book Create Revenue Plans On
Work Order Lead Time Lot Size
eBay End Auctions When Out of Stock
Location Work Order Lead Time Lot Size
Location Work Order Fixed Lead Time
Location High Impact Late Demand Threshold
Vendor Bill - Purchase Order Amount Tolerance
Vendor Bill - Purchase Order Quantity Tolerance
Vendor Bill - Purchase Order Quantity Difference

Vendor Bill - Item Receipt Quantity Tolerance
Vendor Bill - Item Receipt Quantity Difference
Same as Primary Book Rev. Rec.

searchColumns.label: Accounting Book Rev Rec Forecast Rule
Accounting Book Create Revenue Plans On
Work Order Lead Time Lot Size
eBay End Auctions When Out of Stock
Location Work Order Lead Time Lot Size
Location Work Order Fixed Lead Time
Location High Impact Late Demand Threshold
Vendor Bill - Purchase Order Amount Tolerance
Vendor Bill - Purchase Order Quantity Tolerance
Vendor Bill - Purchase Order Quantity Difference
Vendor Bill - Item Receipt Amount Tolerance
Vendor Bill - Item Receipt Quantity Tolerance
Vendor Bill - Item Receipt Quantity Difference
Same as Primary Book Rev. Rec.

fields.label: Accounting Book
fields.help: You must select an accounting book before you select the subsidiary. Only active accounting books appear on the list.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: Select the class that the transaction must be associated with in order to meet the mapping rule.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: Select the department that the transaction must include in order to meet the mapping rule.

fields.label: Destination Account
fields.help:
Select the account that you want transactions post to in the secondary book that you selected from the Accounting Book list.


Select -Same- if you want skip mapping for the set of dimensions you have selected. This enables you to apply the mapping rules to all dimensions except a specific set.

fields.label: Effective Date
fields.help: Enter or select the date that the account mapping begins.

fields.label: Item Account
fields.help:
Select the general ledger account type or other account specified on the Item record in Accounts section of the Accounting subtab.

The item account type you select determines which accounts are available in the Source Account list.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Select the location that the transaction must be associated with in order to meet the mapping rule.

fields.label: Source Account
fields.help:
Select an account that is used for the primary book. This is the account that is mapped from.

The list includes both primary book accounts and secondary book accounts.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: Select the subsidiary that must be associated with the transaction in order to meeting the mapping rule. You must select an accounting book to select a subsidiary.

itembinnumber.scriptingNotes: Available in Records Browser but not in SuiteScript Supported Records

fields.label: Description
fields.help:
Enter a description of the item collection, for example, its purpose and the basis on which members are chosen.

If you see the Enhance text button next to this field, you can get writing help for this field from our generative AI service, NetSuite Text Enhance. Click the Enhance Text button to use the available options. Learn more about Text Enhance.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help: Check this box if you do not want the item collection to be available for use.

fields.label: Name
fields.help: Enter the name of the item collection.

fields.label: Saved Search
fields.help: Select the item saved search you want to use to add items to the item collection.

fields.label: Alternate Source Item
fields.help: The Alternate Source Item field enables you to choose a different item to use that item's historical data to calculate demand.

fields.label: Historical Analysis Duration
fields.help:
In the Historical Analysis Duration field, enter the number of periods in the past to calculate demand projection:
    * Moving Average - enter the number of periods in the past to calculate the next moving average value.
    * Linear Regression - enter the number of periods in the past to determine the linear regression projection.
    * Seasonal Average - enter the number of periods in the past to calculate the buckets in the future.

fields.label: View
fields.help:
You can alter your view of the demand plan by changing the date range or the plan view, as described below:
    * Year – Enter the year to view.
    * Month – Select a month to view
    * Start Date – Enter the first date of the period you want to view.
    * End Date – Enter the last date of the period you want to view.
    * View – Choose to see the demand results in a Daily, Weekly or Monthly format.

fields.label: End Date
fields.help:
You can alter your view of the demand plan by changing the date range or the plan view, as described below:
    * Year – Enter the year to view.
    * Month – Select a month to view
    * Start Date – Enter the first date of the period you want to view.
    * End Date – Enter the last date of the period you want to view.
    * View – Choose to see the demand results in a Daily, Weekly or Monthly format.

fields.label: Item
fields.help: Select the item you are entering a demand plan for.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: If you use the Multi-Location Inventory feature, select a location. Then, the list of items that shows is filtered to show only items for the selected location that are time-phased replenishment items.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Optionally enter a memo for this plan. Later, you can search for this plan by the text entered here.

fields.label: Month
fields.help:
You can alter your view of the demand plan by changing the date range or the plan view, as described below:
    * Year – Enter the year to view.
    * Month – Select a month to view
    * Start Date – Enter the first date of the period you want to view.
    * End Date – Enter the last date of the period you want to view.
    * View – Choose to see the demand results in a Daily, Weekly or Monthly format.

fields.label: Projection Duration
fields.help: In the Projection Duration field, enter the number of periods to calculate projected future demand. For example, enter 6 to calculate demand over a six month period.

fields.label: Projection Interval
fields.help: In the Projection Interval field, select the period of time demand is calculated for. For example, select Weekly to project how much demand is expected per week, as opposed to per month. If you are using the Seasonal Average method, only a Monthly interval is supported.

fields.label: Projection Method
fields.help:
Select a Projection Method:

    * Linear Regression – Use previous demand to project future inventory needs assuming a steady linear growth.
    * Moving Average – Use previous demand to calculate the overall average stock level needed, and then project future stock levels using that overall average.
    * Seasonal Average – Use previous demand to examine the seasonal trend of inventory flow, and then project a similar seasonal trend for future stock levels.
      Note: Using this method, the projection interval must be set to Monthly intervals.
    * Sales Forecast – When using NetSuite for your sales operations, this option uses forward looking sales forecast data to project inventory demand.
      When you use the Sales Forecast method, transaction types sourced for projection calculations are cash sale, invoice, estimate, opportunity, sales order, and item fulfillment.

fields.label: Projection Start Date
fields.help: Once you select a projection interval, the first date of the period you want to forecast demand for is displayed in the Projection Start Date field.

fields.label: Start Date
fields.help:
You can alter your view of the demand plan by changing the date range or the plan view, as described below:
    * Year – Enter the year to view.
    * Month – Select a month to view
    * Start Date – Enter the first date of the period you want to view.
    * End Date – Enter the last date of the period you want to view.
    * View – Choose to see the demand results in a Daily, Weekly or Monthly format.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: Select the subsidiary associated with this item demand plan.

fields.label: Unit of Measure
fields.help: Select a Unit of Measure to create a demand plan based on the unit.

fields.label: Year
fields.help:
You can alter your view of the demand plan by changing the date range or the plan view, as described below:
    * Year – Enter the year to view.
    * Month – Select a month to view
    * Start Date – Enter the first date of the period you want to view.
    * End Date – Enter the last date of the period you want to view.
    * View – Choose to see the demand results in a Daily, Weekly or Monthly format.

itemdemandplan.scriptingLevel: Copy Not Supported, No Available Transforms

fields.label: Ancillary Endorsement
fields.help: For SmartPost Returns, select Address Correction or Return Service for Presorted Standard SmartPost shipments.

fields.label: Certified Mail
fields.help: Indicates this package is being sent by certified mail.

fields.label: Created From
fields.help:
This field shows the sales order this fulfillment is created from.

Click the link to open the sales order.

fields.label: Customer
fields.help:
The customer or job associated with this transaction appears here.

For details on existing customers and projects, go to Lists > Relationships > Customers.

fields.label: Handling Cost
fields.help:
The handling cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field.

To change the cost of handling, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items and select the shipping method with the handling cost you want to change.

fields.label: Inside Delivery
fields.help: Check this box to deliver to a location other than the loading area.

fields.label: Inside Pickup
fields.help: Check this box to pick up the order from a location other than the loading area.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help:
Enter an optional memo with additional information about this fulfillment.
 
What you enter here can be used in searches and shows on reports.

fields.label: Packed Date
fields.help:
Picked

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

Packed

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Pack Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

Shipped

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Pack Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Ship Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

    * The Accounting Period field is required and it is populated based on the Ship Date.

fields.label: Picked Date
fields.help:
Picked

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

Packed

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Pack Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

Shipped

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Pack Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Ship Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

    * The Accounting Period field is required and it is populated based on the Ship Date.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help:
Select the period you want to post this transaction to.

If a period is closed, you cannot post to that period.

fields.label: Requested By
fields.help:
Shows the fulfillment request that the item fulfillment originated from.

Click the fulfillment number to open the fulfillment request.

fields.label: Address
fields.help:
The address this order will be shipped to displays in this field.

    * Select New in the Address Select field to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record.
    * Select Custom in the Address Select field to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record).
    * Click the Edit icon for the Address Select field to edit an existing shipping address.

fields.label: Address Select
fields.help:
Select the address to ship the order to. The full address displays in the Address field.
 
    * Select New to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction and saved with the associated entity record.
    * Select Custom to enter a new shipping address to be used for this transaction only (and not saved with the associated entity record).
    * Click the Edit icon to edit an existing shipping address.

fields.label: Residential Address
fields.help:
Check this box if this address is not a business.

This helps return accurate rates from UPS and FedEx.

fields.label: Total Package Weight
fields.help: This field totals the weight for all packages on this order in pounds.

fields.label: Total Package Weight
fields.help: This field displays the total weight of all individual packages for this order.

fields.label: Shipping Method
fields.help:
Select a shipping method for this order.

If you automatically charge for shipping, the shipping amount is entered in the shipping costs field. To automatically charge for shipping, go to Setup > Accounting > Shipping. Check the Charge for Shipping box and click Save. You must also set up the charge details of your shipping items.

To add or edit shipping items, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items.

fields.label: Shipped Date
fields.help:
Picked

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

Packed

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Pack Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

Shipped

    * If the Pick Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Pack Date field is empty, it populates with the current date.

    * If the Ship Date field is empty, it populates with the current date and the Transaction Date field is populated with the current date.

    * The Accounting Period field is required and it is populated based on the Ship Date.

fields.label: Shipping Cost
fields.help: If you charge for shipping, enter the amount of shipping costs for this order here.

fields.label: Status
fields.help:
Select Picked if you want to pick, pack and ship your orders as separate transactions.

Select Packed if you want to combine the steps to pick and pack orders. Orders will then need to be marked shipped.

Select Shipped if you want to combine the steps to pick, pack and ship orders. Orders will then need to be billed.

fields.label: Terms of Sale
fields.help:
This field is required for dutiable International Express shipments.

Note: This field is not applicable to International document Express shipments.

fields.label: Date
fields.help:
This is the date you are fulfilling this order. By fulfilling this order, you may be shipping items or completing services.

If you want to change the date, enter or click Pick to select a different date.

fields.label: Ref. No.
fields.help:
NetSuite increases the largest fulfillment number by one.

If you wish, you can type another number. The next fulfillment number will revert to the standard pattern.

fields.label: Available to Adv. Partners
fields.help:
Check this box to make this item available to partners with the Advanced Partner Center role for viewing.

These items are also available to partners with roles that are customized for the Advanced Partner Center and include view, create or edit access to item records.

fields.label: Class
fields.help:
Select a class to associate with this item.

Click New to enter a new class record.

Go to Setup > Company > Classes for details about existing class records.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help:
Select the form you want to use to enter this record.

You can select the standard form, a custom form you have already created, or select New to create a custom form.

fields.label: Department
fields.help:
Choose a department to associate with this item.

Click New to enter a new department record.

Go to Setup > Company > Departments for details about existing department records.

fields.label: Description
fields.help:
Enter a description for this item. This description will appear in the Description column of sales forms.

If you see the Enhance text button next to this field, you can get writing help for this field from our generative AI service, NetSuite Text Enhance. Click the Enhance Text button to use the available options. Learn more about Text Enhance.

fields.label: Display Name/Code
fields.help:
Enter an optional name of up to 250 characters for this item in addition to the Item Name. If you are integrating with a Yahoo! store, this field is imported from Yahoo!.

The display name prints in the Item column of sales forms when Basic printing is used. If this item is a member of a kit, this name appears in the Item column when the Print Items box is checked.

If you do not enter a display name, the item name appears on printed forms.

fields.label: Include Children
fields.help:
Check the Include Children box to share the item with all the sub-subsidiaries associated with each subsidiary selected in the Subsidiary field.

Note: When sharing items across subsidiaries, all of the options selected on the item record must be compatible across subsidiaries.
 
For example, when entering an inventory item to be shared across subsidiaries, you should select Income and Asset accounts on the item record that are also shared across the same subsidiaries.

fields.label: Include Start/End Lines
fields.help:
Choose whether the start and end lines of the item group are referenced for sorting picking tickets that include item groups.
    * Clear the Include Start/End Lines box to delete the start or end line of the group on picking tickets. Then, component items for the group are sorted with other items on the picking ticket.
    * Check the Include Start/End Lines box to recognize the start or end line of the group on picking tickets.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help: When you check this box, this item will no longer appear on a list unless you check the Show All box at the bottom of the list page.

fields.label: Product
fields.help:
Select the product this item is associated with.

Product records are tracked with versions and builds for issue management.

To create product records, go to Setup > Issues > Products > New.

fields.label: Is VSOE Bundle
fields.help: Check this box if this group of items is a VSOE bundle. The VSOE price of each item will then be used to allocate revenue across all items sold in the group.

fields.label: Item Name/Number
fields.help:
Enter up to 250 characters for the name of this item. This name appears in lists on transactions.

If you have the option of entering a display name and do not, the item name prints in the Item column of sales forms when Basic printing is used.

If you have the option to enter a vendor name and do not, the item name prints in the Item column of purchase forms. If you have entered a display name, it will print on purchases instead of the item name when Basic printing is used.

Select the type of item you want to print labels for.

Select All to show all types of items.

fields.label: Location
fields.help:
Select a location to associate with this item.
To use a location, the Multi-Location Inventory feature must be enabled.

    * If the Multi-Location Inventory feature is not enabled:

      Selecting an item record location limits the items that certain roles can access.
      For example, Role A is set up to access only items associated with Location One. Therefore, employees who have Role A can access only items associated with Location One.

      Note: If the Multi-Location Inventory feature is not enabled, you cannot use this field to track inventory by locations. For example, you cannot track how many widgets you have in stock in Location One.

      For more information click here.

    * If the Multi-Location Inventory feature is enabled:

      Selecting an item record location classifies the item by that location to limit the items that certain roles can access. To track inventory per location, use the Locations subtab at the bottom of this form.

      For more information click here.

Select New to enter a new location record.

Go to Setup > Company > Locations for details about existing location records.

fields.label: Subitem of
fields.help: If you want this item to be a subitem, select the parent item here.

fields.label: Print Items
fields.help:
Check this box to display the member items with their respective display names, quantities and descriptions on sales and purchase forms. Clear this box to display only the description and amount of the group or kit on sales and purchase forms.

Note: Assembly items do not display on purchase forms even when this box is checked.  Also note that member items are never shown on printed forms; only one line with the group name and summary is printed.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help:
Select one or multiple subsidiaries.
To select multiple subsidiaries, press and hold the Ctrl key while selecting each subsidiary.
A subsidiary must be associated with an item in order for the item to be added to a transaction related to that Subsidiary.

For example, you can enter a sales order and select the Wolfe US subsidiary on the transaction. When you select an item to add to the sales order, only items associated with Wolfe US can be added to the sales order.

fields.label: UPC Code
fields.help:
Enter a maximum of 999 characters in this field to identify an internal name or number to store the item's UPC Code. You can use the text you enter here when adding the item to transactions, searching, or viewing reports.
    * When you enter text in this field, the SKU/UPC field on item labels displays this UPC Code and prints in UPC bar code format.
    * When this field is clear, the SKU/UPC field on item labels displays the Item Name/Number and prints in Code-128 bar code format.

fields.label: Vendor Name/Code
fields.help:
If your vendor uses a name for this item that is different from the name you use, you can enter up to 60 characters as the vendor's name for this item here.

The vendor's name for this item prints in the Item column of purchase forms.

If you do not enter a vendor name, the item name prints in the Item column of purchase forms. If you have entered a display name, it will print on purchases instead of the item name.

fields.label: Firm/Release Advance Warning Threshold
fields.help: Defines a threshold used by the supply planning process to determine when a warning message should be generated for a planned order.

fields.label: ATP Lead Time
fields.help:
Enter an ATP Lead Time to use as a planning horizon for supply and demand considerations in the ship date recommendation calculations.
It also provides a ship date on orders when no inventory is available based on future supply and demand.

fields.label: Backward Consumption Days
fields.help:
When you use the Forecast Consumption demand source method, set the following:
    * Forward Consumption – Number of days after the order date to consider
    * Backward Consumption – Number of days prior to the order date to consider

When backward and forward consumption days are entered for an item, these fields determine the window, or time period, that is considered for each sales order when a forecast amount may be consumed to calculate demand. If an order falls within the consumption window, that order quantity is calculated as being consumed and the forecast is adjusted to account for the order consumption.
Note: NetSuite always considers backward consumption first.  
The forecast closest to the order in the backward window is consumed first.
The forecast closest to the order in the forward window is considered if there are remaining quantities to be consumed.
Note: Only sales order and invoice quantities can consume forecast quantities. Demand from transfer orders and work orders does not consume forecast quantities.

fields.label: Work Order Variable Lead Time
fields.help: Enter the production build time (in days) to build one assembly in the base unit. You can also have NetSuite automatically calculate lead times. Click the Auto Calculate Production Lead Time box.

fields.label: Standard Cost
fields.help:
Enter the price you pay for this item.

If you do not enter a price, purchase orders for this item show the most recent purchase price by default.

If you select a preferred vendor for this item, the price is shown in the currency selected on the vendor's record. If no preferred vendor is selected, the price is shown in your base currency.

fields.label: Costing Lot Size
fields.help:
Enter the Costing Lot Size to display on the assembly item Locations subtab.

The default value for this field is 1 and the minimum value is 0.01.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help:
Select the form you want to use to enter this record.

You can select the standard form, a custom form you have already created, or select New to create a custom form.

fields.label: Default Return Cost
fields.help: Enter the rate you want to default to show as the cost for this item when it is returned. What you enter in this field defaults to show in the Override Rate field on item receipts. You can still change this value after it appears on the item receipt.

fields.label: Demand Source
fields.help:
Select a Demand Source to determine where demand data is sourced for an item.

    * Forecast from Demand Plan – Source only the item's demand plan record.
      Note: A sales order with a related work order generates a supply plan for the sales order. The Mass Create Work Orders page automatically suggests a supply for the sales order.
    * Entered and Planned Orders – Source open orders and use the expected ship date as the demand date. If the item is a member of an assembly, demand for the assembly is included demand calculations for the item.
    * Order and Forecast – Calculates demand for an item by including both the forecast amount and the amount on orders that have been entered.
      Forecast demand for an item is calculated by combining (Quantity forecast over time) + (quantity on sales orders and invoices entered)
    * Forecast Consumption – Calculates demand for an item by subtracting from the forecast quantity any item quantities on orders entered. This removes duplication if an order is already included as part of a forecast.
      Demand for an item is calculated as (Quantity forecast over time) - (quantity on sales orders and invoices entered).

fields.label: Demand Time Fence
fields.help:
Demand Time Fence defaults to the number entered in the Default Demand Time Fence field.
Verify the default or enter a number between zero and 365 to determine the demand time fence for this item.

fields.label: Fixed Lot Size
fields.help: If you selected Fixed Lot Size as the lot sizing method, then enter a quantity in the Fixed Lot Size field. This is the quantity that procurement of this item is always based on, regardless of demand projections.

fields.label: Forward Consumption Days
fields.help:
When you use the Forecast Consumption demand source method, set the following:
    * Forward Consumption – Number of days after the order date to consider
    * Backward Consumption – Number of days prior to the order date to consider

When backward and forward consumption days are entered for an item, these fields determine the window, or time period, that is considered for each sales order when a forecast amount may be consumed to calculate demand. If an order falls within the consumption window, that order quantity is calculated as being consumed and the forecast is adjusted to account for the order consumption.
Note: NetSuite always considers backward consumption first.  
The forecast closest to the order in the backward window is consumed first.
The forecast closest to the order in the forward window is considered if there are remaining quantities to be consumed.
Note: Only sales order and invoice quantities can consume forecast quantities. Demand from transfer orders and work orders does not consume forecast quantities.

fields.label: Inventory Cost Template
fields.help: Select the inventory cost template you want to associate with this item.

fields.label: Classification
fields.help: This field displays the chosen classification used for inventory counts.

fields.label: Count Interval
fields.help: This field displays the total number of days between required counts. For example, if you enter 30, the date a count is required is calculated based on 30 day intervals.

fields.label: Auto Loc. Assignment Allowed
fields.help:
* Check this box to enable the automatic location assignment process to assign the location to lines in a sales order.
      The box for this attribute is checked by default for all locations.
    * If the box is cleared for a location, the location will not be assigned even if it meets all other automatic location assignment rule criteria.
    * To enable the automatic location assignment process to assign a location again, clear this box for the location.
    * This attribute appears in the Locations list in the Purchasing/Inventory subtab.

fields.label: Auto Loc. Assignment Suspended
fields.help:
* Check this box to temporarily suspend the automatic location assignment process from assigning the location to sales order lines.
    * Check this box if you feel that there are discrepancies in inventory at the location.
    * To enable the automatic location assignment process to assign a location again, clear this box for the location.
    * This attribute appears in the Locations list in the Purchasing/Inventory subtab.

fields.label: Item
fields.help:
Enter or select one or more items to determine which items the attributes are copied to.
Click the Select Multiple icon to open the Choose Item window and select more than one item.
Note: If the source item is a different item type than the target item, data in extraneous fields is copied over but does not affect calculations. For example, if the source item processed is a time-phased planning item but the target item is not a time-phased planning item, fields relevant to time-phased planning will be populated on the target item but the data is not used for calculations.

fields.label: High Impact Late Demand Threshold
fields.help: Defines a threshold for highlighting late demand in the workbench as high impact.

fields.label: Lead Time
fields.help:
Lead time is the average number of days between ordering this item from the vendor and receiving it.
    * Auto-Calculating – Check the Auto-Calculate box if you want NetSuite to calculate the lead time based on the most recent order history of this item. Lead time is calculated by taking the number of days between the order entry and receipt on the three most recent purchase orders, and dividing by three. If more than three purchase orders exist, all purchase orders within the period specified in the Order Analysis Interval field on the Inventory Management Preferences dialog will be used.
          o If there are multiple receipts for the item against the same purchase order, the calculation is made using the difference between the purchase order and the last receipt (the receipt that fully receives the order).
          o Lead time calculation is not weighted by number of units received.
          o More recent purchase orders without receipts are ignored.
    * Manually Calculating – Clear the Auto-Calculate box to manually enter a lead time value in days. If the Auto-Calculate box is cleared and no value is entered, then the default value from the Set Up Inventory Management page is used.

Select a location to associate with this item.
To use a location, the Multi-Location Inventory feature must be enabled.

    * If the Multi-Location Inventory feature is not enabled:

      Selecting an item record location limits the items that certain roles can access.
      For example, Role A is set up to access only items associated with Location One. Therefore, employees who have Role A can access only items associated with Location One.

      Note: If the Multi-Location Inventory feature is not enabled, you cannot use this field to track inventory by locations. For example, you cannot track how many widgets you have in stock in Location One.

      For more information click here.

    * If the Multi-Location Inventory feature is enabled:

      Selecting an item record location classifies the item by that location to limit the items that certain roles can access. To track inventory per location, use the Locations subtab at the bottom of this form.

      For more information click here.

Select New to enter a new location record.

Go to Setup > Company > Locations for details about existing location records.

fields.label: Allow Store Pickup
fields.help:
Check the Allow Store Pickup box to enable store pickup.
All items are enabled for store pickup by default.

fields.label: Buffer Stock
fields.help:
In the Store Pickup Buffer Stock field, enter the minimum inventory to be maintained at the location when creating store pickup orders.

NetSuite uses the store pickup buffer stock to calculate the quantity available for store pickup of an item. Visitors to the web store will only be able to create a pickup order if the quantity available for store pickup is greater than zero.

The store pickup buffer stock applies to all items at the location.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Optionally enter a memo. Later, you can search for the text you enter in this field.

fields.label: Minimum Reschedule Days
fields.help: The minimum number of days the supply planning process reschedules a supply order from its current period.

fields.label: Name
fields.help: Enter the item name.

fields.label: Next Count Date
fields.help:
Enter the Next Inventory Count Date.
NetSuite uses this information to calculate when that item needs to be counted.

fields.label: Past Due Forecast Days
fields.help: The number of days into the past the supply planning process will search for past due unconsumed forecast.

fields.label: Periods of Supply Increment
fields.help:
In the Period of Supply Increment field, enter a number from 1 to 90. The default setting is 1.
The increment starts on the first day an order is required. From the first day, NetSuite aggregates all orders in the increment. Orders are placed on the first day of the period.
Note: The Periodic Lot Size Increment field is enabled only when you select Interval in the Periodic Lot Size Type field.

fields.label: Periods of Supply Type
fields.help: The Supply Type field shows the method by which more stock is procured, either Purchase or Assembly. On assembly items, if you have enabled the Allow Purchase of Assembly Items preference, you can choose whether to build additional supply or purchase it.

fields.label: Preferred Stock Level
fields.help:
The preferred stock level is the optimum quantity to maintain in stock of an item.

The quantity you enter here is used to determine your replenishment needs on the Order Items page. It is the quantity you want to have in stock after an order is placed.

    * Auto-Calculating – Check the Auto-Calculate box if you want NetSuite to calculate the preferred stock level based on demand for the item.

      The preferred stock level is calculated as:

      (daily demand * number of days supply preferred).

      If no preferred stock level is identified, then the default preferred stock level is used from the Set Up Inventory Management page.

    * Manually Calculating – Clear the Auto-Calculate box to manually enter the preferred stock quantity.

The preferred stock level you set is used to calculate the quantity of items to be ordered on the Order Items page.

fields.label: Reorder Point
fields.help:
The Reorder Point is the quantity level at which you need to reorder or build more of this item.

    * Auto-Calculating – Check the Auto-Calculate box if you want NetSuite to calculate the reorder point based on demand for the item over time. The reorder point depends on the safety stock definition and is calculated as follows:
          o Without safety stock defined:
            Reorder point = (average lead time days * demand)
          o With safety stock defined in days:
            Reorder point = [(average lead time days + safety stock days) * demand]
          o With safety stock quantity defined:
            Reorder point = [(average lead time days * demand) + safety stock quantity)
    * Manually Calculating – Clear the Auto-Calculate box if you want to manually enter the point at which to reorder or build more of this item.

If you go to Home > User Preferences and place a check mark in the Inventory Level Warnings box, you are reminded when you have reached the reorder point.

fields.label: Reschedule In Days
fields.help:
In the Reschedule In Days field, enter a number between one and 90 that is the maximum number of days that the order can be advanced from the current day. For example, if you enter 10 in this field, an order for this item can be moved up ten days earlier, but not eleven or more days. This field defaults to be blank.
Note: If this field is left blank, NetSuite does not make recommendations to reschedule orders for this item to a later date.

fields.label: Reschedule Out Days
fields.help:
In the Reschedule Out Days field, enter a number between one and 180 that is the maximum number of days that the order can be delayed from the current day. For example, if you enter 10 in this field, an order for this item can be moved to ten days later, but not eleven or more days. This field defaults to be blank.
Note: If this field is left blank, NetSuite does not make recommendations to reschedule orders for this item to a later date.

fields.label: Safety Stock Level
fields.help:
Enter the amount of an item you prefer to keep in stock at all times. Safety stock can be a quantity or a number of days worth of stock.
 
This amount is used to auto-calculate the reorder point of an item.
    * To define safety stock as a quantity, enter a value.
    * To define safety stock as a number of days, enter a value in the field next to Days.

If no safety stock value is entered, then the default value from the Set Up Inventory Management page is used.

Select one or multiple subsidiaries.
To select multiple subsidiaries, press and hold the Ctrl key while selecting each subsidiary.
A subsidiary must be associated with an item in order for the item to be added to a transaction related to that Subsidiary.

For example, you can enter a sales order and select the Wolfe US subsidiary on the transaction. When you select an item to add to the sales order, only items associated with Wolfe US can be added to the sales order.

fields.label: Lot Sizing Method
fields.help:
Select a Lot Sizing Method:

    * Lot For Lot – Orders are suggested for procurement based on the exact projections for that day. The suggested order quantity may vary from day to day depending on demand calculations.
    * Fixed Lot Size – Orders are suggested for procurement based on a fixed amount or a multiple of the fixed amount.
    * Periods of Supply – Generates aggregated purchase orders or work orders based on the overall demand requirements extended over a designated period, such as weekly or monthly.
      For example, rather than creating multiple purchase orders for each instance of demand, you can consolidate into one order created from the demand planning engine for all items required within the next 2 weeks. By sending a consolidated purchase order to a vendor, the vendor can ship all items at one time rather than in multiple shipments, potentially resulting in reduced shipping costs.
      Note: Be aware of costs from vendor holding charges. You can consolidate orders for a period, but after being consolidated, the Bill of Materials (BOM) for that specific work order on that specific level will also be used for subsequent levels.

fields.label: Planning Time Fence
fields.help: This field defaults to the number entered in the Default Planning Time Fence field. Verify the default or enter a number between zero and 365 to determine the planning time fence for this item.

fields.label: Supply Type
fields.help:
In the Supply Type list, select a method for procuring more stock: Purchase or Assembly.
On assembly items, if you have enabled the Allow Purchase of Assembly Items preference, you can choose whether to build additional supply or purchase it.

itemlocationconfiguration.scriptingLevel: Read, Create, Edit, Delete, and Search

fields.label: Description
fields.help: Enter a description for the item process family.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help:
Check this box to inactivate the item process family record.


Inactive records do not appear in lists and cannot be selected on records and transactions.

fields.label: Name
fields.help:
Enter a unique name for the item process family record.

Item process families are groupings of similar products that you can use to identify items that should be stored together in the same area of your warehouse.

fields.label: Description
fields.help: Enter a description for the item process group.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help:
Check this box to inactivate the item process group record.


Inactive records do not appear in lists and cannot be selected on records and transactions.

fields.label: Name
fields.help:
Enter a unique name for the item process group record.

Item process groups are groupings of similar products that use a more refined classification than item process families.

fields.label: Class
fields.help:
Select a class to associate with this transaction.

To create a new class, select New.

For details on existing classes, go to Setup > Company > Classes.

fields.label: Created From
fields.help:
This field shows the purchase order this item receipt is created from.

Click the link to open the purchase order.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: The currency used on the purchase order for this receipt is shown here.

fields.label: Department
fields.help:
Select a department to associate with this transaction.

To create a new department, select New.

For details on existing departments, go to Setup > Company > Departments.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.help: This field shows the name of the entity you are receiving items from.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help:
The currency's exchange rate is shown in this field.
 
You can edit the exchange rate for this transaction only, or you can update the currency record with the exchange rate you enter here.

fields.label: Landed Cost per Line
fields.help: If this box is checked, all new item receipts, credit card charges, vendor bills, and checks default to use landed cost per line.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Enter an optional reference memo for this receipt. Later, you can search for this receipt by text entered in the memo.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help:
Select the period you want to post this transaction to.

If a period is closed, you cannot post to that period.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: This field displays the subsidiary associated with the received items.

fields.label: Date
fields.help:
This is the date you are receiving this order.

If you want to change the date, enter or click Pick to select a different date.

fields.label: Reference #
fields.help: NetSuite increases the largest item receipt number by one. If you wish, you can type another number. The next item receipt number will revert to the standard pattern.

fields.label: Effective Date
fields.help: Enter the revision Effective Date.

fields.label: Inactive
fields.help: Check the Inactive box to not display this revision in lists.

fields.label: Item
fields.help: Select an Item to associate with this revision.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help:
Enter a Memo about this revision.
You can later search for this version by memo text.

fields.label: Name
fields.help: Enter a revision Name. For example, Version One.

fields.label: Obsolete Date
fields.help:
Obsolete dates on a revision record cannot be edited. You can set the effective date and when the revision is saved.

The obsolete date is determined by NetSuite to avoid gaps or overlaps in dates covered by revisions.

fields.label: Item
fields.help: Select the item to create a supply plan for.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: If you use the Multi-Location Inventory feature, select a location. Then, the list of items that shows is filtered to show only items for the selected location that are time-phased replenishment items.TEST

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Optionally enter a memo. You can later search for this plan using this text.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: If you use NetSuite OneWorld, select a Subsidiary.

fields.label: Unit of Measure
fields.help: Select a Unit of Measure to create a supply plan based on the unit.

projecticchargerequest.scriptingNotes: Available in Records Browser but not in SuiteScript Supported Records

fields.label: Accounting Book
fields.help: Select an accounting book. You may select the primary book or any secondary accounting book to which you have access.

fields.label: Approval Status
fields.help: Select the approval status for this journal entry.

fields.label: Approved
fields.help: Check this box to indicate that this journal entry is approved. If checked, this journal entry will post immediately.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: If this entry is associated with a particular class, select it.

fields.label: Void Of
fields.help: This field shows the original transaction that is being voided.

fields.label: Credit
fields.help: The total amount of credits you enter below appears here. If the difference between the Debit and Credit fields is not equal to 0.00, NetSuite will not allow you to submit this transaction.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: This field is the currency used for all amounts in this journal entry.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help: Select the custom journal entry record, if required.

fields.label: Debit
fields.help: The total amount of debits you enter below appears here. If the difference between the Debit and Credit fields is not equal to 0.00, NetSuite will not allow you to submit this transaction.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: If you like, select a department.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help: This field displays the default exchange rate.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Enter a memo to describe this journal entry. It will appear on this transaction detail as well as reports such as a 2-line account register.

fields.label: Next Approver
fields.help: Select the user required to approve this journal entry.

fields.label: Nexus
fields.help: Select the Nexus, if any, associated with this journal entry.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help: The current period is entered as the posting period for the journal entry.

fields.label: Reversal Date
fields.help: If this journal entry is intended to be reversed, enter the date for the reversing entry to be posted.

fields.label: Defer Entry
fields.help: Check this box to make the reversal a memorized transaction that automatically occurs on the date entered in the Reversal Date field.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: Select the subsidiary to associate with this journal entry.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help: Select the tax registration number, if any, associated with this journal entry.

fields.label: To Subsidiary
fields.help: Choose the second subsidiary for this ledger transaction.

fields.label: Date
fields.help: NetSuite inserts today's date as the date of this journal entry.

fields.label: Entry No.
fields.help: NetSuite increases the largest journal entry number by one. Max 45 characters.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: If you like, select a department.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Select a location to associate with this line item.

fields.label: Available to Adv. Partners
fields.help: Check this box to make this item available to partners with the Advanced Partner Center role for viewing. These items are also available to partners with roles that are customized for the Advanced Partner Center and include view, create or edit access to item records.

fields.label: Billing Schedule
fields.help: Choose the billing schedule you want to associate with this item. When an item is associated with a billing schedule, the billing schedule appears by default when the item is added to an order. Note: Forms must be customized to show schedules on lines. Read Applying Billing Schedules. Click New to enter a new billing schedule.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: Select a class to associate with this item. Click New to enter a new class record. Go to Setup > Company > Classes for details about existing class records.

fields.label: Eligible For Contingent Revenue Handling
fields.help: Check this box to indicate that the item is subject to contingent revenue handling. When checked, revenue allocation is affected.

fields.label: Item Defined Cost
fields.help: Enter an Item Defined Cost amount.

fields.label: Cost Estimate Type
fields.help: The Cost Estimate Type determines what value NetSuite uses to calculate estimated Gross Profit. The estimated Gross Profit for Items on a transaction provides the data needed to calculate the total estimated Gross Profit on that transaction. The individual line items that you enter in a transaction determine the amounts that post when you process that transaction. The following Cost Estimate Types on Items are available: * Item Defined Cost - a user-defined amount, entered into the Item Defined Cost field on the Item definition page. * Average Cost - NetSuite calculates an average cost of the units purchased. * Last Purchase Price - This field displays the most recent purchase price of the item as determined by purchase order receipt transactions. * Purchase Price - Price entered that you pay for this item. If you do not enter a price, then the most recent purchase price from purchase orders provides the price for this item by default. * Preferred Vendor Rate - This option is only used if the Multi-Vendor feature is enabled and multiple vendors supply the same item. o First priority is to use the preferred vendor rate if defined on the Item record. o Next priority would be to use the purchase price. o Last priority would be the purchase order rate. (Initially this uses the preferred vendor rate cost, and then after a purchase order is entered, this type uses the most recent actual purchase order rate. Special orders and drop-shipped items use this cost information.) * Derived from member items - Total costs of items currently included in a kit. This Cost Estimate Type only applies to kits and sums the estimated costs of each item in the kit, based on each of their individual Cost Estimate Types. Uses the latest definition of the kit, not its historical definition.

fields.label: Manufacturer Country
fields.help: Enter the name of the country where this item is manufactured. This information is used for customs purposes when shipping.

fields.label: Create Revenue Plans On
fields.help: Select the event that triggers creation of revenue recognition plans. Match the event with the amount source of the item's revenue recognition rule. * Revenue Arrangement Creation - Revenue plans can be created when the revenue arrangement is created. Use this option with rules that have Event-Amount as the Amount Source. * Billing - Revenue plans can be created when the sales order is billed and from stand-alone cash sales, invoices, credit memos, and cash refunds. Use this option with rules that have Event-Percent based on amount as the Amount Source. * Fulfillment - This option is available only when Advanced Shipping is enabled. Revenue plans can be created upon fulfillment. Use this option with rules that have Event-Percent based on quantity as the Amount Source. * Project Progress - This option is available only when the Projects feature is enabled. Use this option with rules that have Event-Percent Complete as the Amount Source.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help: Select the form you want to use to enter this record. You can select the standard form, a custom form you have already created, or select New to create a custom form.

fields.label: Deferred Revenue Account
fields.help: Select the deferred revenue account to associate with this item. If you use revenue recognition or advanced revenue management, the revenue from the sale of this item is deferred.

fields.label: Hold Revenue Recognition
fields.help: Check this box to delay recognizing revenue from the sale of this item. When this box is checked, revenue recognition schedules or revenue plans are created with the status On Hold. For more information, see the help topic Delaying Revenue Recognition for an Item.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: Choose a department to associate with this item. Click New to enter a new department record. Go to Setup > Company > Departments for details about existing department records.

fields.label: Description
fields.help: Enter a description for this item. This description will appear in the Description column of sales forms.

fields.label: Direct Revenue Posting
fields.help: Check this box to disable advanced revenue management for this item. When checked, posting transactions that include this item post directly to the item revenue account. No revenue element or revenue arrangement is created. When you check this box, the Deferred Revenue Account on the Accounting subtab is disabled. When you create sales transactions that include items that have this box checked, all the items in the transaction must have the box checked. You cannot mix items that post directly to revenue with items that post to deferred revenue in the same transaction. This restriction also applies to kit items. All items in a kit must post either to revenue or to deferred revenue. You cannot check or clear the box after the item has been used in a transaction with advanced revenue management. By default, this box is not checked.

fields.label: Display Name/Code
fields.help: Enter an optional name of up to 250 characters for this item in addition to the Item Name. If you are integrating with a Yahoo! store, this field is imported from Yahoo!. The display name prints in the Item column of sales forms when Basic printing is used. If this item is a member of a kit, this name appears in the Item column when the Print Items box is checked. If you do not enter a display name, the item name appears on printed forms.

fields.label: Don't Show Price
fields.help: Check this box to hide the price of this item online. This is useful for items you want to advertise but don't want to sell or for items that you track inventory for and want to display but are offered in combination with other items.

fields.label: End Auctions When Item is Out of Stock
Enforce Internally
fields.help: Check this box to apply the minimum quantity restriction on sales orders generated from NetSuite. When you clear this box, but enter a number in the Minimum Quantity field, the minimum quantity is only applied to web store orders.

fields.label: Exclude from Sitemap
fields.help: Check this box to exclude a tab, category or item page from the site map.

fields.label: Featured Description
fields.help: If this item is displayed on the home page of your Web site, enter a featured description for this item. This description appears below the item's store display name on the Home page of your store or site. You can enter up to 999 characters including basic HTML code. Enter and format text using the formatting options, or click the HTML Source Code link to enter HTML.
Check this box to include this item when exporting item lists to Froogle. Clear this box to remove this item when exporting item lists to Froogle.

fields.label: Handling Cost
fields.help: As an option, you may enter a handling cost for this item in dollars. You must also create a shipping item for per-item shipping and handling costs at Lists > Shipping Items > New. When this item is selected on sales orders, invoices or cash sales, the appropriate shipping and handling charges are automatically calculated.

fields.label: Include Children
fields.help: Check the Include Children box to share the item with all the sub-subsidiaries associated with each subsidiary selected in the Subsidiary field. Note: When sharing items across subsidiaries, all of the options selected on the item record must be compatible across subsidiaries. For example, when entering an inventory item to be shared across subsidiaries, you should select Income and Asset accounts on the item record that are also shared across the same subsidiaries.

fields.label: Income Account
fields.help: Select the income account to associate with this item. When no income account is selected, the item does not show in the Item dropdown list for sales transactions.

fields.label: Internal ID
fields.help: Use this ID number to create smart tags for your item templates, site templates, and custom Web site that you host with NetSuite. Smart tags use the following format:

fields.label: Variable Amount
fields.help: Check this box to set this item as variable-priced. This enables customers to enter their own prices for this item, such as for donations.

fields.label: Can be Fulfilled/Received
fields.help: Check the Can be Fulfilled/Received box to enable this item to be received and fulfilled during order processing.
