fields.label: Department
fields.help: If you like, select a department.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Select a location to associate with this line item.

fields.label: Account
fields.help: Select the Accounts Payable account that will be affected by this transaction.

fields.label: Applied
fields.help: NetSuite will update the Applied amount as you check bills on the Apply tab.

fields.label: Auto Apply
fields.help: Check the Auto Apply box if you want NetSuite to apply the credit amount to the oldest outstanding vendor bills.

fields.label: Vendor Select
fields.help: In the Vendor Select field, choose a vendor. Verify the billing address displayed in the Vendor field.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: Select a class to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Created From
fields.help: This field displays the return authorization from which this bill credit is created.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: Select the currency you want to enter this credit in.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: Select a department to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Due Date
fields.help: This field specifies a due date for the bill credit. You can use it to track due dates of bill credit at time of bill payment.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.help: Select the vendor for this credit.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help: This field displays the selected vendor's tax registration number.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help: The currency's exchange rate is shown in this field.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Select a location to associate with this line item.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Enter a memo that will appear on such reports 2-line Accounts Payable Register.

fields.label: Nexus
fields.help: This field shows the nexus of the transaction. NetSuite automatically populates this field based on the nexus lookup logic.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help: Select the period you want to post this vendor credit to.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: This field displays the subsidiary associated with this transaction.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help: This field shows the tax registration number of the transaction nexus.

fields.label: Tax Details Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the tax information on the Tax Details subtab of the transaction.

fields.label: Tax Point Date
fields.help: Tax point date is a legal requirement in many countries. It is the date of the transaction that determines the taxability.

fields.label: Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the tax point date.

fields.label: Tax Registration Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the values in the Nexus and Subsidiary Tax Reg. Number fields.

fields.label: To Be Printed
fields.help: Check this box to save this bill credit in a queue of bill credits to be printed later.

fields.label: Date
fields.help: NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this vendor credit.

fields.label: Reference No.
fields.help: Enter up to 45 characters to identify this transaction, such as the vendor's invoice or credit number.

fields.label: Transaction Number
fields.help: This field displays the system-generated number associated with this transaction.

fields.label: Unapplied
fields.help: NetSuite will update the Unapplied amount as you check bills on the Apply tab.

fields.label: Amount
fields.help: You can enter the total amount of the transaction, but NetSuite will update this as you add or edit line items. The total amount cannot be negative.

fields.label: Account
fields.help: Select the bank account that will be used to pay these bills.

fields.label: A/P Account
fields.help: Select the Accounts Payable account that will be affected by this transaction.

fields.label: Approval Status
fields.help: In the Approval Status field, choose Approved, Pending Approval, or Rejected.

fields.label: Available Balance
fields.help: The Available Balance is displayed for the selected account. Bank account balance minus sum of outstanding in-transit payments.

fields.label: Balance
fields.help: NetSuite shows you the balance in this bank account.

fields.label: Bill Pay
fields.help: Check this box to send this payment online.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: Choose a class to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: Select the vendor's transaction currency you want to enter this bill in.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: This vendor's currency is shown in this field. All currency amounts on this transaction are shown in this currency.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help: Select the form to use for this payment.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: Choose a department to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Payee
fields.help: Select the payee for this purchase. Select a vendor to pay vendor bills. Select an employee to pay an employee expense.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help: The currency's exchange rate is shown in this field.

fields.label: In-Transit
fields.help: Check the In Transit box to designate this vendor payment as an In-Transit Payment. Cannot be changed after the form is saved.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Select a location to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: Enter a memo that shows the vendor's invoice number or your account number. This appears on the memo line of the check.

fields.label: Next Approver
fields.help: This field displays the employee designated as the approver on the employee record.

fields.label: Posting Period
fields.help: Select the period you want to post this bill payment to. If a period is closed, you cannot post to that period.

fields.label: Voucher
fields.help: Check this box to queue a voucher to print for this payment.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: In a OneWorld environment, NetSuite displays the name of the subsidiary associated with the bill in a read-only field.

fields.label: To ACH
fields.help: Check this box to send this payment via ACH processing.

fields.label: To Be Printed
fields.help: Check this box if you wish to save this in a queue of checks to print.

fields.label: Amount
fields.help: NetSuite will update this amount as you add or edit line items.

fields.label: Date
fields.help: NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this bill payment.

fields.label: Check #
fields.help: If you have clicked To Be Printed, you will choose starting check numbers later, when you print those checks.

fields.label: Transaction Number
fields.help: This field displays the system-generated number associated with this transaction.

vendorprepayment.scriptingLevel: Read, Create, Update, Delete, and Search

vendorprepaymentapplication.scriptingLevel: Read, Transform, Update, Delete, and Search

fields.label: Actual Production End Date
fields.help: The Actual Production End Date field is automatically populated based on the latest date of the related Completion (after the Close transaction entry) or Assembly Build transactions.

fields.label: Actual Production Start Date
fields.help:
The Actual Production Start Date field is automatically populated based on the earliest date of the related Issue Components, Completion, or Assembly Build transactions

To override an actual production date, check Enter Manually. Select a new date from the calendar and then click Save.

fields.label: Assembly
fields.help:
Select the Assembly you need to build.

After an item is selected, the assembly components are displayed on the Items subtab.
This field cannot be changed after an assembly build is associated with this work order.

fields.label: Auto-calculate Lag
fields.help:
Check the Auto-Calculate Lag box if you want NetSuite to calculate lag times for operation tasks.
For more information, see Operations Overlap.

fields.label: Bill of Materials
fields.help: If you use Advanced Bills of Materials, the related Bill of Materials is automatically selected.

fields.label: Bill of Materials Revision
fields.help: If you use Advanced Bills of Materials, the related BOM Revision is automatically selected based on the revision effective dates and production start date (the date for the backward scheduling method).

fields.label: Class
fields.help:
Select a Class to associate with the assemblies on this work order.
This selection is carried over to the assembly build.

Click New to set up a new class.

fields.label: Created From
fields.help:
The Created From field displays the number of the sales order associated with this work order, if any.

Note: The Created From field is not available if you are creating a stand-alone work order on the Enter Work Orders page. The Created From field appears only on existing work orders created from sales orders using one of these methods:
    * By clicking on the Work Ord. link on the line item in the sales order
    * By automatic work order creation from a sales order when the Create WO field is marked upon saving the sales order

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help: In the Custom Form list, select the form to use with this work order.

fields.label: Department
fields.help:
Select a Department to associate with the assemblies on this work order.
This selection is carried over to the assembly build.

Click New to enter a new department.

fields.label: Production End Date
fields.help:
The Production End Date defaults to the production start date plus lead time.

When you use Routing you can set the Scheduling Method to Forward (production end date is computed automatically) or Backward (production start date is computed automatically based on the selected production end date).

fields.label: Customer
fields.help: Select a Customer to associate with this work order.

fields.label: Build Sub-Assemblies
fields.help:
Check the Mark Sub-Assemblies Phantom box to build member items that are also assemblies used to complete the work order.
Sub-assembly components are displayed on the Items subtab.

To learn more, see Work Orders and Sub-Assemblies

fields.label: Firmed
fields.help:
Planned work order status is displayed as either Open or Firmed.

   1. Firmed planned work orders are not deleted before supply planning runs.
Check this box to firm the order.
   2. Open planned work orders are deleted before supply planning runs.
Clear this box to keep the order open.

fields.label: Project
fields.help: Select a job if the assemblies on this work order are for a particular job. Click the Open icon to open a list of jobs.

fields.label: Location
fields.help:
Select a Location to display only work orders for that location. This location is also displayed in the assembly build.
If you use the Multi-Location Inventory, component inventory items are committed from the selected location.

To learn more, see Mass Creating Work Orders.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help:
Optionally, enter a Memo for this work order.
You can search for the memo text to find this work order.

fields.label: Status
fields.help:
Select the work order Status.
For example, In-Process or Built.

fields.label: Quantity
fields.help:
Enter the Quantity of assembly items you want to create.
This can be a fractional number.

fields.label: Supply Required By Date
fields.help: The Supply Required By Date represents the date the item must be shipped to the customer.

fields.label: Revision
fields.help:
For work orders, NetSuite automatically populates the effective revision based on the effective date. If you change the revision selected on the work order, then NetSuite changes the components on the top level assembly based on the revision selected.

If you change the revision selected on the work order and the Build Subassembly box is checked, then top-level components will change based on the revision selected and the lower level components are determined based on the effective date.

fields.label: Choose Team
fields.help: In the Choose Team field, select a sales team to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Scheduling Method
fields.help:
Routing and Demand Planning generate supply work orders that enable you to select one of the following methods NetSuite uses to calculate production requirements:

    * Forward Scheduling - set a production start date. NetSuite calculates the time, materials, and resources required to complete all necessary operations to finish the task. The production end date is determined based on these calculations.
    * Backward Scheduling - set the production end date. This is the date items need to be completed. NetSuite calculates the time, materials, and resources required to complete all necessary operations. The production start date is determined based on these calculations.

To learn more, see Production Scheduling Methods Overview.

fields.label: Production Start Date
fields.help:
The Production Start Date automatically defaults to the current date. Depending on your settings, the current date will reflect your location or subsidiary time zone.

Production start date automatically defaults to the current day in your location time zone, if it is set. Otherwise, NetSuite will default to the subsidiary time zone if it is set. If neither location or subsidiary are set as default time zones, NetSuite defaults to your company time zone.

fields.label: Status
fields.help: The operation sequence Status. For example, Not Started, Started, Completed.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: If you use OneWorld, select a subsidiary.

fields.label: Update Customer
fields.help: Check the Update Customer box to advise the sales team on the when the customer's record changes are made here.

fields.label: Update Customer
fields.help: Check the Update Customer box to update the sales team on the customer's record with changes made here.

fields.label: Date
fields.help:
NetSuite displays today's Date as the default work order date.
You can enter or select another date.

fields.label: Order #
fields.help:
NetSuite automatically increases the largest work Order # (number) by one.
You can enter another number, however the next order number will revert to the standard pattern.

fields.label: Units
fields.help: The Units field displays the units of the component used in the parent assembly.

fields.label: Date Created
fields.help: This field displays the creation date of the wave transaction.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: This field displays the warehouse location of the orders in the wave.

fields.label: Wave #
fields.help: This field displays the autogenerated number assigned to a new wave.

fields.label: Status
fields.help:
Select the status of an unreleased wave:

   1. Pending Release - Generate pick tasks and save the wave.
   2. Released - Generate pick tasks and release the wave.

For released waves, this field displays the current status of the wave transaction as the pick tasks are processed.

fields.label: Picking Type
fields.help:
Select the picking type you want to apply to orders in the wave.

   1. Single Order - Generate a pick task for each item line of an order.
   2. Multiple Order - In a single pick task, combine similar items from all released orders in a wave. You can pick the quantity of an item per order.
   3. Bulk Picking - You can pick the entire quantity of an item in bulk across all orders released in a wave. The app distributes the quantities per order.

fields.label: Priority
fields.help:
Select the priority number, from 1 (top priority) to 9 (lowest priority).

On a mobile device, high priority waves appear at the top of the list.

fields.label: Released Date
fields.help: This field displays the release date of the wave.

fields.label: Status
fields.help: This field displays the status of the wave.

fields.label: Wave Type
fields.help: This field displays the transaction type of orders included in the wave.

wave.scriptingLevel: Read, Create, Update, Delete, and Search

website.scriptingNotes: Not supported in beforeLoad user event scripts

winlossreason.scriptingNotes: Available in Records Browser but not in SuiteScript Supported Records

workflowactionscript.scriptingNotes: Script definition record - managed via SDF

fields.label: Approval Status
fields.help:
When you use SuiteFlow for purchase approvals, purchase orders and requests include the Approval Status field. The Approval Status field reflects the state of the transaction in the approval process.

Purchase orders and requests may have one of the following statuses:

    * Pending Approval – The purchase has not yet been approved.
    * Approved – The purchase has been given approval for processing.
    * Rejected – The purchase approval is refused and the order will not be processed.
You may manually select a different status in this field if needed.

fields.label: Available Vendor Credit
fields.help: If you have customized the purchase order to display the Available Vendor Credit field, changing the subsidiary changes the available credit limit value to reflect the currency of the selected subsidiary.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.help: By default, NetSuite inserts the vendor mailing address from the vendor record in the Vendor Select field.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: If you wish, select the class that applies to this purchase order.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: This field displays the vendor currency used for this transaction.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help: If you customized a purchase order form, select it here, or click New to customize a new purchase order form.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: If you like, select a department.

fields.label: Receive By
fields.help: Enter the date by which the items to be ordered are needed.

fields.label: Employee
fields.help: If you are entering a purchase request for an employee, select the employee's name here.

fields.label: Vendor
fields.help: Select the vendor for this purchase.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help: Select the vendor's tax registration number associated with this purchase order.

fields.label: Exchange rate
fields.help: The currency's exchange rate is shown in this field.

fields.label: FOB
fields.help: Enter the location where you technically acquire ownership.

fields.label: Incoterm
fields.help: Select the standardized term for this transaction.

fields.label: Intercompany Status
fields.help: Intercompany Transaction Status.

fields.label: Paired Intercompany Transaction
fields.help: Identifies the intercompany order in another subsidiary that this order is paired with.

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Select a location to associate with this purchase.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: If you wish, enter a memo to describe this order.

fields.label: Vendor Message
fields.help: If you wish, type a message that will appear on the Purchase Order form.

fields.label: Next Approver
fields.help: When you use SuiteFlow for purchase approvals, purchase orders and requests include the Next Approver field.

fields.label: Nexus
fields.help: This field shows the nexus of the transaction.

fields.label: Vendor #
fields.help: NetSuite inserts your account number for this vendor.

fields.label: Purchase Contract
fields.help: This field displays the purchase contract associated with this order.

fields.label: Ship To
fields.help: When you select a customer in the Ship To field in the header of this transaction, the customer's default shipping address autofills this field.

fields.label: Ship Date
fields.help: Enter the date you expect the items to be shipped.

fields.label: Ship Via
fields.help: Select your shipping method for this transaction.

fields.label: Ship To
fields.help: Select the customer you are shipping this order to.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: This field displays the subsidiary associated with this transaction.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help: This field shows the tax registration number of the transaction nexus.

fields.label: Supervisor Approval
fields.help: If you have permission to approve this purchase order, check the Supervisor Approval box.

fields.label: Tax Details Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the tax information on the Tax Details subtab of the transaction.

fields.label: Tax Point Date
fields.help: Tax point date is a legal requirement in many countries.

fields.label: Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the tax point date.

fields.label: Tax Registration Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the values in the Nexus and Subsidiary Tax Reg. Number fields.

fields.label: Terms
fields.help: Select the discount terms that will apply to this order.

fields.label: To Be E-mailed
fields.help: Check this box if you want to e-mail this purchase order.

fields.label: To Be Faxed
fields.help: Check this box if you want to fax this purchase order.

fields.label: To Be Printed
fields.help: Check this box if you wish to save this in a queue of purchase orders to print.

fields.label: Total
fields.help: NetSuite computes the total of line items and then enters it here.

fields.label: Additional Tracking #
fields.help: Enter the shipment tracking number from the shipping company.

fields.label: Date
fields.help: NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this purchase order.

fields.label: PO #
fields.help: NetSuite increases the largest purchase order number by one.
The internal ID of the transaction line item.
The subtype of the item.
The type of the item.
The line ID number of the sublist items on a record.
This is often the same as the line ID number, but linenumber is always sequential without gaps.

fields.label: Handling Cost
fields.help: The handling cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field. To change the cost of handling, go to Lists > Shipping Items and select the shipping method with the handling cost you want to change.

fields.label: Total (Alt. Sales)
fields.help: The alternate sales amount total is shown here.

fields.label: Shipping Cost
fields.help: The shipping cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field above. To change the cost of a shipping method, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items and select the shipping method you want to change. If you use UPS Real-Time rates, shipments over 150lbs are broken up into shipments less than or equal to 150lbs for charging.

fields.label: Bill To
fields.help: The default billing address autofills this field from the customer's record at Lists > Relationships > Customers. To enter a different address: * Select another address in the Bill To Select field. * Select New in the Bill To Select field to enter a new billing address.

fields.label: Bill To Select
fields.help: Select the appropriate billing address for this transaction. Select New to enter a new billing address.

fields.label: Billing Schedule
fields.help: Choose the billing schedule you want to associate with this transaction. When you select a billing schedule, all items on the order are billed according to the billing schedule. Click New to enter a new billing schedule. When you create a new billing schedule on-the-fly, it can be identified as Public or Private. Private billing schedules show only in the transaction it is created from. If you create a private billing schedule from an estimate, that schedule is attached to items on the resulting sales order. Go to Lists > Accounting > Billing Schedules for details about existing billing schedules.

fields.label: Class
fields.help: If you wish, select the class that applies to this estimate. Click New to set up a new class. Go to Setup > Company > Classes for details about existing classes.

fields.label: Coupon Code
fields.help: If you are applying a single-use coupon to this transaction, enter that coupon code here. If you are applying a multiple-use coupon to this transaction, select the promotion in the Promotion field, and the coupon code is shown here.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: If you do not use the Multi-Currency Customers feature, the currency of the customer you are creating this transaction for is shown here. If you use the Multi-Currency Customers feature, select the transaction currency you want to create this transaction in. This customer's primary currency is selected by default. If you have already selected items on this transaction, selecting a new currency refreshes them to show the price in the new currency (except for items you entered custom prices for) and recalculates tax and gross profit.

fields.label: Currency
fields.help: This customer's currency is shown in this field. All currency amounts on this transaction are shown in this currency.

fields.label: Custom Form
fields.help: Select the customized estimate form to use.

fields.label: Department
fields.help: If you like, select a department. Click New to enter a new department. Go to Setup > Company > Departments for details about existing departments.

fields.label: Discount
fields.help: You can select a discount for this estimate. NetSuite fills the Rate and Discount Total fields based on the discount you choose. Leave this field blank if you do not want to apply a discount to this transaction. To create new discount items, go to Lists > Accounting > Items > New. On the New Item page, click Discount.

fields.label: Rate
fields.help: NetSuite enters the rate for the discount item you selected. You can change the discount rate for this cash refund. Enter the discount as a dollar amount like 10.00, or as a percentage like 10%.

fields.label: Discount
fields.help: NetSuite enters the amount discounted on this estimate. If this discount item is taxable, the discount is applied before taxes. If it is not taxable, the discount is applied after taxes.

fields.label: Expires
fields.help: Type or pick a date this estimate expires.

fields.label: End Date
fields.help: Enter or pick the end date for this estimate.

fields.label: Customer
fields.help: Select the customer for this estimate. If you are using jobs, select the appropriate job rather than the master customer account. Click New to set up a new customer.

fields.label: Status
fields.help: Accept or change the status of this estimate. What appears in the Probability field is based on the status selected. If this estimate is for a prospect, the prospect's record is updated with what is entered here. To change the default status of estimates, go to Setup > Sales > Sales Preferences and select a new status in the Prospect Status field.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help: Select the entity's tax registration number associated with this estimate.

fields.label: Est. Gross Profit
fields.help: Estimated Gross Profit: Read-only field that equals the revenue amount minus the Est. Cost. At the transaction level, it equals the gross profit of all lines, factoring transaction-level discounts and markups.

fields.label: Est. Gross Profit Percent
fields.help: Estimated Gross Profit Margin, as a percentage: Read-only field that equals the Est. Gross Profit divided by revenue, expressed as a percentage. At the transaction level, it equals the gross profit percent of all lines, factoring transaction-level discounts and markups.

fields.label: Exchange Rate
fields.help: The currency's exchange rate is shown in this field. You can edit the exchange rate for this transaction only, or you can update the currency record with the exchange rate you enter here.

fields.label: Exp. Close
fields.help: Enter the date you expect this estimate to close.

fields.label: FOB
fields.help: Enter the location where the customers technically acquire ownership. For example, if your customers own goods as soon as they ship and pay the shipping costs from your location to theirs, you might enter Our warehouse.

fields.label: Forecast Type
fields.help: Choose which forecast category this estimate belongs in. If you do not want this estimate to appear in forecasts, select Omitted.

fields.label: Handling Cost
fields.help: The handling cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field. To change the cost of handling, go to Lists > Accounting > Shipping Items and select the shipping method with the handling cost you want to change.

fields.label: Tax Rate
fields.help: The tax rate for this handling shipping item is shown here.

fields.label: Handling Tax Code
fields.help: Select the tax code for this handling shipping item.

fields.label: Enable Item Line Shipping
fields.help: Check this box if the transaction has multiple shipping routes so NetSuite can calculate the correct amounts for each line item.

fields.label: Taxable
fields.help: Place a check mark in this box if the order you are quoting will be taxable.

fields.label: Project
fields.help: Select the project this estimate is associated with.

fields.label: Lead Source
fields.help: The lead source for this transaction is shown here. You can choose a different lead source for this transaction. The default lead source is determined by the Default Lead Source on Sales Transactions preference at Setup > Marketing > Marketing Preferences

fields.label: Location
fields.help: Choose the location you want to associate with this transaction. Select New to enter a new location record. Go to Setup > Company > Locations for details about existing location records.

fields.label: Memo
fields.help: If you wish, enter a memo to describe this estimate. It will appear on reports such as the 2-line Estimates register that your customers can see if you give them permission to log in and view their transaction history.

fields.label: Customer Message
fields.help: The text of the customer message you select in the Select Message field appears here. You can edit the message or enter a new customer message for only this transaction.

fields.label: Select Message
fields.help: Select a customer message to appear on this form. For example, you may add "Thank you for your business!" Choose from the list of existing customer messages stored at Setup > Accounting > Accounting Lists. The text of the selected message appears in the Customer Message field. In this field, you can optionally edit the message or enter a new customer message for only this transaction. To add messages to appear in the Select Message field, go to Setup > Accounting > Accounting Lists > New, and click Customer Message.

fields.label: Nexus
fields.help: This field shows the nexus of the transaction. NetSuite automatically populates this field based on the nexus lookup logic. You can override the transaction nexus and tax registration number that NetSuite automatically selects by checking the Nexus Override box. When you select a different nexus in the dropdown list, the corresponding tax registration number is automatically selected in the Subsidiary Tax Reg. Number field.

fields.label: Opportunity
fields.help: If you are entering this transaction for an opportunity, select the opportunity. You can create opportunity records at Transactions > Sales > Create Opportunities.

fields.label: PO #
fields.help: If a purchase order number applies to this estimate, enter it here.

fields.label: Partner
fields.help: The partner associated with this customer appears in this field. You can choose a different partner to associate with this transaction.

fields.label: Probability
fields.help: Accept or change the probability that this estimate will be converted to a sale. The Probability field shows the probability of the status selected. If this estimate is for a prospect, the prospect's record is updated with what is entered here. To change the default probability of a status, go to Setup > Sales > Customer Statuses, and click the status you want to change.

fields.label: Promotion
fields.help: If you want to apply a multiple-use coupon code to this transaction, select the promotion here. If you want to apply a single-use coupon code, enter the code in the Coupon Code field. The promotion for that coupon code is shown here. Customers associated with a partner can only use coupon codes from that partner's promotions. If you select a promotion associated with a partner, this transaction is attributed to that partner for reporting purposes. You can choose to have a promotion selected by default at Setup > Marketing > Marketing Preferences in the Default Promotion Code on Sales Transactions field. Create new promotion codes at Lists > Marketing > Promotions > New.

fields.label: Choose Team
fields.help: Select a sales team to associate with this transaction. To create a sales team, go to Lists > Relationships > New > Select Sales Team Members.

fields.label: Sales Rep
fields.help: NetSuite inserts the sales representative associated with this company on the customer record at Lists > Customers. If a different sales rep is assigned to this transaction, select that sales rep here. To add additional sales reps to this list, go to Lists > New Employee.

fields.label: Ship To
fields.help: The default shipping address autofills this field from the customer's record at Lists > Customers. To enter a different address: * Select another address in the Ship To Select field. * Select New in the Ship To Select field to enter a new billing address.

fields.label: Ship To Select
fields.help: Select the appropriate shipping address for this transaction. Select New to enter a new shipping address.

fields.label: Ship Date
fields.help: Enter, pick or accept the date you expect to ship items ordered.

fields.label: Ship Via
fields.help: Select a shipping method for this estimate. If you automatically charge for shipping, the shipping amount is entered in the shipping costs field. To automatically charge for shipping, go to Setup > Accounting > Shipping, and check the Charge for Shipping box. Click Submit. You must also set up the charge details of your shipping items. To add or edit shipping items, go to Lists > Shipping Item.

fields.label: Shipping Cost
fields.help: The shipping cost automatically calculates depending on the shipping method you select in the Ship Via field. If this field reads ReCalc, click the Recalculate Shipping button to receive a shipping cost. If you use UPS Real-Time rates and a package is over 150lbs, the package is charged as multiple packages equal to or less than 150lbs.

fields.label: Tax Rate
fields.help: The tax rate for this shipping item tax code is shown here.

fields.label: Shipping Tax Code
fields.help: Select the tax code to apply to this shipping item.

fields.label: Start Date
fields.help: If there is an expected closing date for this estimate, accept today's date or enter a new date here.

fields.label: Subsidiary
fields.help: This field displays the subsidiary associated with this transaction.

fields.label: Tax Reg. Number
fields.help: This field shows the tax registration number of the transaction nexus. NetSuite automatically populates this field based on the nexus lookup logic. You can override the transaction nexus and tax registration number that NetSuite automatically selects by checking the Nexus Override box. When you select a different tax registration number in the dropdown list, the corresponding nexus is automatically selected in the Nexus field.

fields.label: Subtotal
fields.help: This field shows the total of the items on this sales order. This is the total before any discounts, shipping cost, handling cost or tax is added to the order.

fields.label: Update Customer
fields.help: Check this box if you want to update the partner team on the customer record with changes you make here.

fields.label: Update Customer
fields.help: Check this box if you want to update the sales team on the customer's record with changes you make here.

fields.label: Tax Details Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the tax information on the Tax Details subtab of the transaction. Only roles with at least the Edit level of the Tax Details Tab permission can override the tax details.

fields.label: Tax Registration Override
fields.help: Check this box to override the values in the Nexus and Subsidiary Tax Reg. Number fields. Only roles with at least the Edit level of the Tax Details Tab permission can override the values that NetSuite automatically selects in these fields.

fields.label: Tax Total
fields.help: NetSuite multiplies the tax rate by the taxable total of line items and enters it here.

fields.label: Terms
fields.help: Select discount terms. If this estimate is later turned into a sale, the terms set here autofill on the sales form.

fields.label: Title
fields.help: Enter a title for this estimate. The estimate title text appears on lists, shows in search and reporting results, and shows on the Estimates subtab of records it is associated with. When an estimate is converted from an opportunity, the title from the opportunity defaults into the title field on the estimate. If there is already an estimate against the opportunity, then the second estimate has a number following the title. For example, if the first estimate title is “Project Estimate”, then the second estimate title is “Project Estimate1”. For each subsequent estimate created against that opportunity, the number in the title increases by 1 to help distinguish between multiple estimates associated to a single opportunity. The estimate title field is also available for customization, direct list editing, and mass updates.

fields.label: To Be E-mailed
fields.help: Check this box if you want to email this estimate. Then enter the email address in the space to the right of the check box. You can enter multiple email addresses by separating them with semicolons. It is important to separate the addresses with only semicolons, and do not include any spaces.

fields.label: To Be Faxed
fields.help: Check this box if you want to fax this estimate. Enter the fax number in the space to the right of the check box if it doesn't already appear. To fax NetSuite forms, an administrator must first set up fax service at Setup > Company > Printing, Fax and Email Preferences.

fields.label: To Be Printed
fields.help: Check this box if you wish to save this in a queue of estimates to print. Otherwise, you can click Print below to submit and print this estimate at once.

fields.label: Total
fields.help: NetSuite computes the total of line items and tax and then enters it here.

fields.label: Est. Extended Cost
fields.help: Estimated Cost: Estimated cost of the specific number of items; estimated rate x quantity = estimated cost.

fields.label: Additional Tracking #
fields.help: Enter the shipment tracking number from the shipping company. If a tracking number is entered in the exact format used by UPS or FedEx and the transaction is saved, the tracking number will show on the transaction as a link to the UPS or FedEx website. You can click the link to check the status of the shipment.

fields.label: Date
fields.help: NetSuite inserts today's date for the posting date of this estimate. You can type or pick another date. What you enter here determines the date range in which this transaction appears on the estimate registers. You can select or enter custom date ranges on registers.

fields.label: Estimate #
fields.help: NetSuite increases the largest estimate number by one. If you wish, you can type another number. The next estimate number will revert to the standard pattern.

fields.label: Available In Customer
fields.help: Check the Available in Customer Center box to make this estimate available to customers in the Customer Center. Clear this box to leave this estimate off the list of estimates available to customers in the Customer Center. This field is checked by default.

fields.label: Tax
fields.help: Place a check mark in this box if the order you are quoting will be taxable.
